titlestmt.author: Compiled by Jane Meditz, with the assistance of Anne Rhodes

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 4, 2025 at 20:00

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Original music manuscripts, personal writings (e.g. correspondence, essays, plays, poems), and ephemera.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 161, Irving Schlein Papers in the Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is divided into five series: I. Music, II. Writings, III. Correspondence, IV. Playbills and Concert Programs, V. Photographs

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Irving Schlein (1905-1986) was an American composer of orchestral, chamber, instrumental, choral and operatic works A lifelong New Yorker, he also enjoyed a 30-year career on Broadway as a musical director, pianist, arranger, and composer.

He held degrees in piano from New York College of Music ('28) and Juilliard School of music ('30) and was one of the last two living students of Franz Liszt. He later studied composition with Wallingford Reigger, Roger Sessions, Aaron Copland, and Roy Harris.

Many of Schein's compositions reacted to events and American world history -- such as slavery, World War II, and the Holocaust -- as well as everyday life. He was known for his collaborations with Kurt Weill, and also worked Al Jolson, Cole Porter, and Lerner and Lowe. Later in life, he taught music in the New York City school system, producing his vocal works, including the folk opera "Stackalee," with his students. He transcribed the 1867 Aleen, Ware and Garrison collection of "Slave Songs of the United States." In 1968, he completed his full-length dramatic opera, "Salammbo." Schlein died in his native Manhattan in 1986 at the age of 80.

did.unittitle: Andante for Recorder, Pardessus de Viole, and Viola d'Amore
Berceuse and Dance for Viola d'Amore and Piano
"The Mighty Bowl" - Pardessus de Viole and Viole da Gamba
Petite Suite pour Viole d'Amour et Clavier
Sonatine pour Viole d'Amour and Harpsichord
Berceuse I and II for Violin and Piano
"The Mighty Bowl" Transcribed for Piano and Violin
Petite Suite for Viola and Piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 31. One version of opening of Fugue is listed as the first movement of String Quartet No. 2.

did.unittitle: Unidentified (possibly excerpt from "Fugue for String Quartet?")
Duet for Bb Instruments (for Two Trumpets)
Encore Piece for Flute and Piano
In Modo Classico for Flute and Horn
Mozartean Duet for Clarinet and Trumpet
Petite Suite for Two Flutes and Horn
A Piece in the Classic Mode (for Two Flutes)
Six Short Pieces for Flute and Horn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 5. Full score and partial flute part.

did.unittitle: Theme from (Postlude to) "Salambo" for Horn and Piano
Three Movements for Two Oboes and Piano
Three Short Pieces for Trumpet and Trombone
Trio for Horn, Bassoon, and Piano
One Movement for Horn, Oboe, and Strings
Trio for Flute, Clarinet, and Viola

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 125. Top of first page also says "'I Have a Dream' - May 1968

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 81. Top of first page also says "Symphony No. 4"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 6. Original title on front page is "Symphony # 2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 143. Original title in inventory was "Symphony no. 5 for Symphonic Band." Physical item says "no. 8." Same piece?

did.unittitle: Give Me the Splendid Silent Sun for Narrator and Orchestra
Grand Central Terminal - A Symphonic Burlesk

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 132. Draft/ piano reduction and full score.

did.unittitle: Immigrants all…Americans all - Suite for Orchestra
Ode to Victory for Orchestra - dedicated to the United Nations
Pioneer 10 Pushes Beyond Goals, Into the Unknown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 139. Pencil draft/ piano reduction and full score.

did.unittitle: Prelude from "The Bell and The Light" Opera
Song of the Broad-Axe for Chorus and Orchestra
Spirit of the Union for Chorus and Orchestra or Band

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 142. Piano reduction and full score.

did.unittitle: Testament to Freedom for Soloists, Chorus, and Orchestra
A Musical Escapade for Two Pianos
"Oklahoma"- arranged for two pianos by Schlein
Common School - A Study of English Literature
Basso Ostinato and Toccata and Fugue for Organ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 215. Related to Prelude for Hammond Organ

did.unittitle: Andante and Allegro for Organ and Orchestra
Concerto No. 1 for Piano and Orchestra
Concerto No. 2 for Piano and String Orchestra ("Opus Two")
Dance Suite for Horn and Orchestra
A Musical Escapade for Piano and Orchestra

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 182. Partial draft of score and set of parts.

did.unittitle: The Bell and the Light (Negro Folk-Opera)
Morning Exercises for All the Year, A Day Book for Teachers
Morning Exercises for All the Year, Musical Calendar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ISSM 211. Bound photocopied version and several drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Likely connected to "My Heart's in the Highlands."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Full score (ISSM 212), piano-vocal score (ISSM 213). Additional materials, including drafts and typed libretto.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple versions/ materials, including piano score (ISSM 159) and orchestral score (ISSM 207).

did.unittitle: Slave Songs of the United States

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings on different musical genres/ styles, and treatises on music theory.
